description: git diff の変更ファイルから関連テストのみ自動選択して高速実行する（フルテスト実行より大幅短縮）。USE WHEN 修正直後の素早い動作確認、impl-orchestrator や code-review 起動前の事前チェック、コミット前の軽量検証。SKIP リリース前のフルテストスイート、未コミット変更がない場合（git diff で対象が拾えない）。

results.query: I just patched 3 files in src/auth/ (login.ts, session.ts, oauth.ts). Run only the tests related to those changes — full suite is too slow right now and I just need a sanity check before commit.
Before I open this PR for the discount calculation refactor, can you sanity-check the diff with whatever tests are relevant? Don't run the full suite, just the ones that touch my changes.
Made some quick edits to handlers/order.ts and validators/order_validator.ts, want to make sure I didn't break anything obvious before I commit and push to the PR branch.
Use git diff to find what I touched in this last hour and run only those tests, please. Speed > coverage right now — I have a meeting in 10 min.
fast test plz — ive only changed like 4 lines in handlers/payment.ts and i wanna make sure regression doesnt bite me before i commit
Run only the tests related to my git diff changes. Full suite is too heavy right now and I'm just doing a quick patch on the validator helpers.
Before committing this small refactor in src/utils/format.ts, just run whatever tests cover that file or its callers — full suite would take 10 min and I don't have it.
just changed handlers/order.ts and the related schema in schemas/order.ts. quick test on those touched bits before commit pls

Before opening this PR for the order validation refactor (touched 4 files in src/order/handlers/ and 2 in tests/order/), I want a quick regression check on whatever tests are relevant — full suite is overkill here.
Run diff-based selective tests right now. I'm about to commit and have no time for the full pipeline — just the affected ones.
Run the entire test suite, including integration tests and e2e, for the release preflight check on tag v2.3.0.
There's a failing test in tests/auth.test.ts — fix the bug that's causing it to fail and verify with the regression tests.
What testing framework should I use for a Rust project — cargo test, nextest, or something else? I care about speed and parallel execution.
Write a new test case for the divide-by-zero edge case in src/calculator.ts — the existing tests don't cover it.
Generate test data for a 100-row CSV file with realistic-looking customer addresses and order amounts.
Pre-release verification — run the entire test suite end-to-end on tag v2.3.0 including integration tests and e2e on the staging environment.
Mock the database connection in tests/order.test.ts — currently it hits the real Postgres which is flaky in CI.
Add boundary test cases (0, MAX, MIN, NaN) for the decimal handling in src/decimal.ts — current tests don't cover those edge values.
Recommend a mocking library — torn between jest mocks, vitest mocks, and sinon for our TypeScript project. We're on Vitest right now.
Explain TDD best practices for a junior dev who just joined the team. Looking for a 1-page summary.
